name: Sourdough Starter
description: A self-perpetuating wild yeast culture from flour and water that provides free leavening indefinitely — also breaks down phytic acid and partially pre-digests gluten through fermentation.
replaces: Commercial yeast, artisan bread purchases ($3–8 per loaf)
effectivenessNote: Produces superior bread with complex flavor, better texture, and improved digestibility. Takes 5–7 days to establish but lasts forever. Requires weekly maintenance.
warnings: Takes 5–7 days of daily feeding to become active
Discard half before each feeding (or use discard in recipes)
Maintain in fridge with weekly feeds if not baking frequently
Whole wheat or rye flour establishes faster than white flour
scienceNote: Wild Saccharomyces and Lactobacillus species colonize the flour-water mixture. Lactobacilli produce lactic and acetic acids (the sour flavor) while yeasts produce CO₂ for leavening. The acidic environment breaks down phytic acid (an anti-nutrient) and partially denatures gluten proteins, improving mineral bioavailability and digestibility.
